title: Refactor a manual parser into a recursive binary-matching dispatch
prompt:
Given:

```
def decode_list(binary) do
  decode_list(binary, [])
end

defp decode_list("", acc), do: Enum.reverse(acc)
defp decode_list(binary, acc) do
  len = :binary.first(binary)
  payload = :binary.part(binary, 1, len)
  rest = :binary.part(binary, 1 + len, byte_size(binary) - 1 - len)
  decode_list(rest, [payload | acc])
end
```

Rewrite the private helper using binary pattern matching in the function head: `defp decode_list(<<len::8, payload::binary-size(len), rest::binary>>, acc)`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.LenPrefixList do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Synthesizes binary pattern matching + recursion. The `len::8, payload::binary-size(len)` combo is the key idiom.
